behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies: correctness depends on real speech-model diarization/transcription quality (speaker-label preservation), which a stub would hide (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets a single stage output: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels," not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real audio with multiple known speakers through the actual speech-to-text API and assert speaker labels remain correctly attached to utterances used downstream for attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS indicates local parsing/structuring logic; failure is in extraction rules, not infra or model behavior. Rejected Integration because behavior text is function-level: "Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments... into the summary input structure" with no required module boundary or real I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures containing explicit assignments and assert extracted `{assignee, task_text}` entries in the summary-input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output content (coverage of assignees in generated action items), so deterministic stubs can pass while real summaries fail (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because this assertion can be evaluated at the summarization stage with transcript/input context; behavior text does not require validating multi-agent pipeline sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Using real transcript inputs with known assignees, call the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic routing/mapping logic after decisions are already identified; no real OS/network/model dependency is required. Rejected Agentic due disqualifying text "identified decisions" and focus on "routes ... into the `decisions` section schema" (post-identification formatting behavior).
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision records, verify mapper outputs them only under `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Behavior checks semantic extraction/placement quality of real summary output from transcript content, which depends on model judgment (LLM-DEP). A mocked model would mask misses (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because behavior wording starts from "mentioned in the transcript" and validates summary placement, not end-to-end multi-agent chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcripts containing explicit decisions, invoke the real summarizer, and assert those decisions are represented in `decisions` rather than only narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP applies: sequential segment processing plus merge is explicitly a chained pipeline. It also involves real model outputs across segments where coherence can degrade, so lower-level stubs hide failures. Rejected Agentic because behavior text explicitly requires sequence orchestration ("splits ... processes sequentially, merging results").
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through the production segmentation path, execute segment-by-segment transcription/summarization, then verify merged final summary coherence and structured completeness.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly end-to-end, multi-stage, and includes real infra + real model calls across steps (upload -> transcribe -> summarize -> deliver). Failures are at pipeline handoff/order boundaries and cannot be exposed by isolated tests. Rejected System because behavior text requires full chained agentic pipeline, not just one real-infra boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like run with a real meeting file and assert unattended completion plus valid structured output containing expected sections and schema.
